name: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset
description: The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service (server resource consumption) via a stream cancellation attack.
root_cause: Improper handling of RST_STREAM frames allowing rapid generation of requests without waiting for responses.
attack_flow: Attacker opens a large number of HTTP/2 streams
Attacker immediately sends a RST_STREAM frame for each stream
The server processes the request but cancellation happens too fast for resource cleanup
Server CPU and memory are exhausted processing pending requests

fix.patch: Apply patches for web servers (Nginx, HAProxy, etc.).
fix.configuration: Limit the number of concurrent streams and RST frames per connection.
steps: Update load balancers and web servers to latest versions
Implement rate limiting for RST_STREAM frames
Monitor connection state table sizes

name: Citrix NetScaler RCE
description: An unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway.
root_cause: Stack-based buffer overflow in the processing of authentication requests.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a specially crafted authentication request
Large payload overflows the stack buffer during string processing
Attacker gains control of the instruction pointer
Arbitrary code execution with root privileges on the device
impact: Full takeover of the Citrix gateway and access to internal network.

fix.patch: Upgrade Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway to latest firmware.
steps: Install critical security updates from Citrix
Check for indicators of compromise (web shells in /var/vpn/)
Rotate all credentials stored on the device

name: Ivanti Connect Secure RCE
description: A command injection vulnerability in web components of Ivanti Connect Secure.
root_cause: Insufficient sanitization of user-supplied parameters in the administrative web interface.
attack_flow: Attacker authenticates (or bypasses via CVE-2023-46805)
Attacker sends a request with shell metacharacters in parameters
The server executes the injection via a system() call
Attacker gains persistent access as a root user
impact: Full compromise of VPN infrastructure and eavesdropping on sessions.

fix.patch: Apply the latest Ivanti security patches.
fix.configuration: Import the XML mititgation file provided by Ivanti.
steps: Apply Ivanti vendor security updates
Run the Integrity Checker Tool provided by vendor
Monitor for unauthorized administrative logins

name: XZ Utils Backdoor
description: A malicious backdoor was introduced into XZ Utils versions 5.6.0 and 5.6.1.
root_cause: Sophisticated multi-year social engineering campaign targeting the project maintainer.
attack_flow: Malicious code is hidden in binary test files during build time
The backdoor hijacks the RSA_public_decrypt function in sshd
Attacker sends a specific public key that triggers code execution
Unauthorized RCE/Authentication bypass on SSH servers
impact: Complete compromise of Linux distributions using the affected versions.

steps: Audit systems for XZ Utils version 5.6.0/5.6.1
Force downgrade to a known good version (e.g. 5.4.6)
Check for binary mutations in the sshd process

name: Jenkins CLI File Read
description: The Jenkins CLI command parser allows unauthenticated users to read arbitrary files from the server.
root_cause: The 'args4j' library used by Jenkins expands '@' followed by a file path into the file content.
attack_flow: Attacker uses the Jenkins CLI with a payload like 'connect-node @/etc/passwd'
Jenkins expands the filename into its content during argument parsing
The file content is leaked in an error message or console output
Sensitive credentials or configuration details are exposed
impact: Theft of SSH keys, admin credentials, and confidential configs.

fix.configuration: Disable the CLI feature if not required.
steps: Update Jenkins controller to the latest version
Disable access to the CLI via Global Security settings
Verify no sensitive files were accessed via logs

name: Apache Struts File Upload RCE
description: An attacker can manipulate file upload parameters to enable path traversal and achieve RCE.
root_cause: Improper validation of parameter case and path traversal characters in file upload logic.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a multipart/form-data request
Parameter name is manipulated using case variation (e.g., 'Upload')
Attacker uses ../ sequences in the filename
Malicious file is written to an executable directory (e.g., webapps)
impact: Remote code execution on the application server.

steps: Upgrade the Struts2 library in project dependencies
Restrict write permissions for the application user
Use a WAF to filter traversal patterns in filenames

name: Apache ActiveMQ RCE
description: An attacker with network access to the OpenWire port can execute arbitrary commands.
root_cause: Insecure deserialization of OpenWire commands during transportation.
attack_flow: Attacker connects to the ActiveMQ broker (Port 61616)
Attacker sends a malicious OpenWire command containing a class name
The broker instantiates the class using reflection
Arbitrary code is executed during instantiation
impact: Complete compromise of the message broker and hosted messages.

fix.configuration: Block the OpenWire port from public access.
steps: Update ActiveMQ distribution to a patched version
Enable authentication for all transport connectors
Audit broker for unauthorized web shells

name: NSO Pegasus Blastpass
description: A buffer overflow in the ImageIO framework allows unauthenticated RCE via a malicious image.
root_cause: Integer overflow in the parsing of maliciously crafted images.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a malicious PassKit (.pkpass) attachment via iMessage
The device automatically processes the image attachment
Vulnerability triggers during ImageIO parsing
Zero-click installation of Pegasus spyware
impact: Silent surveillance and data theft from mobile devices.

fix.configuration: Enable Lockdown Mode on high-risk devices.
steps: Install latest iOS and iPadOS updates
Enable Lockdown Mode for sensitive users
Monitor for suspicious outgoing network traffic

name: WebP Heap Buffer Overflow
description: A heap buffer overflow in the libwebp library when processing lossless images.
root_cause: Memory corruption during Huffman table construction for WebP images.
attack_flow: Attacker creates a maliciously crafted WebP image
User opens the image in a browser or viewer using libwebp
Vulnerability triggers a heap overflow during decompression
Attacker achieves code execution within the application context
impact: Remote code execution in browsers, image viewers, and backend services.

steps: Update all browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
Rebuild applications using affected libwebp versions
Patch OS-level image processing libraries

name: runc Container Escape
description: An attacker can escape container isolation and gain host-level access.
root_cause: Leaky file descriptor in runc allows access to the host file system during process startup.
attack_flow: Attacker runs a malicious image or uses a specific working directory
runc inadvertently leaks an internal file descriptor (e.g. /proc/self/cwd)
Attacker uses the fd to access the host's /proc filesystem
Attacker gains full access to the host filesystem
impact: Full compromise of the container host and all other containers.

fix.configuration: Avoid running untrusted images as root.
steps: Update Docker/Kubernetes runtime to use runc 1.1.12+
User user namespaces to mitigate impact of escapes
Scan container images for malicious 'WORKDIR' instructions
